evidence_quotes.quote: they're considered "licenses" or "services" and not products
these 'licenses' have been taken away from the client/consumer for unjustifiable reasons

once purchased, digital goods such as videogames, music, and movies, should fully belong to the client/consumer, and these goods shouldn't be able to be taken away at any time the person providing them wants

paragraphs.text: I've read through the act and I firmly support it, and I believe the general idea is great, it is certainly true that specially in the digital world which is relatively new and it's very unregulated limits have to be placed, shady market practices are often excercised agaisnt the rights of the client/consumer. But one suggestion I want to add is regarding the topic of the ownership of videogames. When you buy videogames in the digital world, they're considered "licenses" or "services" and not products, this has led to various cases where these "licenses" have been taken away from the client/consumer for unjustifiable reasons. I believe that this act could use a point stating that once purchased, digital goods such as videogames, music, and movies, should fully belong to the client/consumer, and these goods shouldn't be able to be taken away at any time the person providing them wants, this is heavily tied to an initiative that I please ask (if possible) that is also taken into consideration here. This initiative I'm talking about wants to tackle the issue of the digital products I've mentioned before being "destroyed" by the providers, for example, forcing their users to constantly make use of their servers, and when they shut the servers down, they make the goods unusable. Maybe the Digital Fairness Act and that initiative could be tied together. To give you an idea of how many Digital goods have been destroyed as I mentioned prior, I will leave a link here of a list of videogames that different people have decided to contribute to. If you cannot read through it it's fine. https://stopkillinggames.wiki.gg/wiki/Dead_game_list I want to end this feedback saying thanks for letting me express my opinions this way and for letting me directly speak about how I feel about the laws and acts that after all, will affect our lives, and I hope that my feedback wasn't too invasive, or excessive.
